seo.title: Sensitive Data Leakage Prevention in AI Agents in Amazon Bedrock Agents Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix sensitive data leakage prevention in ai agents in Amazon Bedrock Agents agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: Sensitive Data Leakage in Amazon Bedrock Agents occurs when PII, API keys, or proprietary data from knowledge bases or action groups gets exposed in agent responses. This manifests through malformed Lambda action responses, unfiltered knowledge base retrievals, and guardrails failing to catch domain-specific leaks. Bedrock's multi-turn session state can also propagate leaked data across conversations, amplifying exposure in enterprise deployments.

howItManifests.scenario: Action group Lambda functions returning malformed responses
howItManifests.symptom: Lambda returns raw database records with PII in action group output
howItManifests.impact: Customer SSNs, emails exposed in agent final response

howItManifests.scenario: Knowledge base retrieval missing relevant documents due to chunking
howItManifests.symptom: Agent pulls irrelevant docs containing sensitive employee data
howItManifests.impact: Internal HR data leaked to external users

howItManifests.scenario: Agent guardrails not catching domain-specific policy violations
howItManifests.symptom: Guardrails block generic profanity but miss API keys in responses
howItManifests.impact: Live AWS credentials exposed to end users

howItManifests.scenario: Session state loss between multi-turn agent invocations
howItManifests.symptom: Previous turn's leaked PII cached in sessionAttributes
howItManifests.impact: Data compounds across conversations, violating GDPR

detection.name: Scan Agent Responses for PII Patterns
detection.description: Detects SSNs, emails, and credit cards in Bedrock agent output using regex patterns

detection.name: Monitor Lambda Action Group Outputs
detection.description: Intercepts action group responses for raw sensitive data before agent processing

detection.name: Session State PII Auditor
detection.description: Scans Bedrock sessionAttributes for accumulated sensitive data across turns
detection.code:
import json
import boto3

bedrock = boto3.client('bedrock-agent-runtime')

def audit_session_state(session_id):
    # Get session state
    response = bedrock.get_agent_session(
        agentId='YOUR_AGENT_ID',
        agentAliasId='latest',
        sessionId=session_id
    )
    
    session_attrs = json.loads(response['sessionState']['sessionAttributes'])
    
    risky_keys = ['customer_data', 'pii', 'credentials']
    leaks = []
    
    for key, value in session_attrs.items():
        if any(risky in key.lower() for risky in risky_keys):
            leaks.append({'key': key, 'value_preview': str(value)[:100]})
    
    return {'leaks_found': len(leaks), 'details': leaks}

fixes.name: Lambda Action Group PII Filter
fixes.description: Sanitizes Lambda responses before returning to Bedrock agent

fixes.name: Knowledge Base Response Filter
fixes.description: Post-processes KB retrievals to strip sensitive chunks

fixes.name: Custom Bedrock Guardrail Config
fixes.description: Configures domain-specific content filters beyond default guardrails

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Sensitive Data Leakage Prevention in AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex provides continuous runtime verification of Bedrock Agents by wrapping invoke_agent() calls with vex.verify(). The platform automatically detects PII leakage, malformed action responses, and session state violations using pre-built detectors trained on Bedrock trace patterns. 

When leakage is detected, Vex applies remediation policies like response redaction, session termination, and automatic guardrail updates. Integration takes <5 minutes and provides enterprise-grade observability across all agent deployments.

Vex's Bedrock-specific detectors catch the unique failure modes like action group response parsing errors and knowledge base chunk leakage that generic monitoring misses.
vexAutomation.code:
import vex
import boto3

# Install: pip install boto3 vex-sdk

bedrock = boto3.client('bedrock-agent-runtime')

@vex.verify(
    detectors=[
        'pii-leakage',
        'bedrock-action-group-malformed',
        'session-pii-accumulation',
        'kb-sensitive-retrieval'
    ],
    remediation='redact-and-log',
    policy='block-on-leak'
)
def safe_agent_invoke(agent_id, session_id, input_text):
    '''Vex-wrapped Bedrock agent invocation'''
    response = bedrock.invoke_agent(
        agentId=agent_id,
        agentAliasId='latest',
        sessionId=session_id,
        inputText=input_text
    )
    
    # Vex automatically scans response.trace, sessionState, and completion
    return response

# Usage
result = safe_agent_invoke('YOUR_AGENT_ID', 'sess-123', 'Show me customer data')

# Vex returns:
# {
#   'verified': True/False,
#   'issues': [...],
#   'redacted_response': {...},
#   'policy_action': 'allowed/blocked'
# }
